LAW AND MOTION 301, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT JUDICIAL COUNCIL OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED WITH 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. ON DEMURRER, THE COURT CANNOT ACCEPT AS TRUE A CONTRACT NEITHER ATTACHED TO THE COMPLAINT NOR THE SUBJECT OF A PROPER R.J.N. NOR DOES THE COURT ACCEPT AS TRUE CROSS-DEFENDANT'S PREFERRED INTERPRETATION OF THE CONTRACT. CROSS-DEFENDANT IS DIRECTED TO READ AND COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6(C). OPPOSING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189 (301/PJB)

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-21-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PETER A. KRAUSE, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. (916) 324-5328 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-DEFENDANT JUDICIAL COUNCIL OF CALIFORNIA;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT JUDICIAL COUNCIL OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is overruled with 10 days to answer. On demurrer, the Court cannot accept as true a contract neither attached to the complaint nor the subject of a proper R.J.N. Nor does the Court accept as true cross-defendant's preferred interpretation of the contract. Cross-defendant is directed to read and comply with Local Rule 2.6(C). Opposing party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =(301/PJB)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-28-2010 Continued To Jul-30-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-26-2010 Continued To Jun-11-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212
